\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Polar $(\rho, \theta)$ quadrature generator on a triangular facet. 

Workflow\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Construct with a triangle, an observation point, and a 1D Gauss rule (nodes/weights) of size {\ttfamily n\+Quadrature} for both $\rho$ and $\theta$ expansions.
\item The constructor calls \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_abb4388c4a651d097c0c93376c45010e3}{adapt\+Polar()} which\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Projects geometry to the triangle plane (\doxylink{classTriQuadPol_aea4deef1068d5095c5c6afbb741be8d7}{transform\+In\+Plane()}).
\item Determines angular sectors and their radial bounds (\doxylink{classTriQuadPol_aa0a0a7af46125d5359ae00fca0621de2}{adapt\+In()} if the point lies inside the facet\textquotesingle{}s projection, else \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a3f18d2c370480a52cb1364f9dd95ed06}{adapt\+Out()}).
\item Assembles barycentric quadrature points/weights (\doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a70845b7e565aa1befcffd099f850215b}{assemble\+From\+Polar()}).
\end{DoxyItemize}
\item Retrieve integration nodes/weights via \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a937d9b0718e7914525a47db7954a1ef4}{get\+Quad\+Points()}/\doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a23097391f76d79ed0e0a3584400d4497}{get\+Quad\+Weights()}. 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{TriQuadPol_8h_source_l00036}{36}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{TriQuadPol_8h_source}{Tri\+Quad\+Pol.\+h}}.

void Tri\+Quad\+Pol\+::adapt\+In (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{double}]{theta0}{, }\item[{double}]{theta\+Start}{, }\item[{double}]{theta\+End}{, }\item[{double}]{t\+Line}{, }\item[{double}]{d\+Line}{, }\item[{std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{int\+Thetas}{, }\item[{std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{int\+Weights}{, }\item[{std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{min\+Radius}{, }\item[{std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{max\+Radius}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Build angular bins and radial bounds when the point is inside the triangle. 

Uses a single boundary line per angular sector.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em theta0} & Global rotation added to all output angles. \\
\hline
{\em theta\+Start,theta\+End} & Sector angular limits. \\
\hline
{\em t\+Line,d\+Line} & Line parameters from \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a143954247825794d0dc5c0d0e3d98365}{line\+In\+Polar()} $\{\theta_0, d_0\}$. \\
\hline

\hline
{\em min\+Radius,max\+Radius} & Output inner/outer radii for each $\theta$ bin.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Build a θ-\/bin and radial bound for the inside case (single edge).

Fills {\ttfamily int\+Thetas/int\+Weights} with Gauss nodes/weights on \mbox{[}θ\+\_\+start, θ\+\_\+end\mbox{]}, and sets r\+\_\+max(θ) = d0 / cos(θ − θ0), r\+\_\+min = 0.

Build angular bins/radial bounds when the point is outside the triangle. 

Uses two boundary lines per angular sector (enter/exit).


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}

\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Build a θ-\/bin and two radial bounds for the outside case (two edges).

Sets r\+\_\+min/max(θ) from two edge lines; swaps if min \texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} max numerically.

{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classTriQuadPol_abb4388c4a651d097c0c93376c45010e3} 
void Tri\+Quad\+Pol\+::adapt\+Polar (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Build angular sectors and radial bounds, then assemble nodes/weights. 

Calls, in order\+: \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_aea4deef1068d5095c5c6afbb741be8d7}{transform\+In\+Plane()} -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_aa0a0a7af46125d5359ae00fca0621de2}{adapt\+In()}/\doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a3f18d2c370480a52cb1364f9dd95ed06}{adapt\+Out()} -\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>} \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a70845b7e565aa1befcffd099f850215b}{assemble\+From\+Polar()}. Safe to call multiple times (clears previous results).

Main driver\+: project to plane, split angular sectors, and assemble nodes/weights.

Clears previous results, then\+: 1) \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_aea4deef1068d5095c5c6afbb741be8d7}{transform\+In\+Plane()} 2) Decide inside/outside and compute sector data via \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_aa0a0a7af46125d5359ae00fca0621de2}{adapt\+In()}/adapt\+Out() 3) \doxylink{classTriQuadPol_a70845b7e565aa1befcffd099f850215b}{assemble\+From\+Polar()}

{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classTriQuadPol_a70845b7e565aa1befcffd099f850215b} 
void Tri\+Quad\+Pol\+::assemble\+From\+Polar (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{in\+Thetas}{, }\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{in\+Weights}{, }\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{in\+Min\+Radii}{, }\item[{const std\+::vector$<$ double $>$ \&}]{in\+Max\+Radii}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Expand $\theta$-\/bins into ( $\theta, r$) Gauss products and convert to barycentric nodes. 


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em in\+Thetas} & Vector of $\theta$ nodes (bin centers). \\
\hline
{\em in\+Weights} & Vector of $\theta$ weights. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Min\+Radii} & Vector of $r_{min}$ per $\theta$ node. \\
\hline
{\em in\+Max\+Radii} & Vector of $r_{max}$ per $\theta$ node.\\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
Expand (θ,r) Gauss products and convert to barycentric coordinates.

Each θ-\/bin is expanded by n\+Quadrature radial nodes. The Jacobian factor r and the radial span (r\+\_\+max -\/ r\+\_\+min) are included in the weights.

\Hypertarget{classTriQuadPol_a143954247825794d0dc5c0d0e3d98365}\index{TriQuadPol@{TriQuadPol}!lineInPolar@{lineInPolar}}
\index{lineInPolar@{lineInPolar}!TriQuadPol@{TriQuadPol}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{lineInPolar()}{lineInPolar()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classTriQuadPol_a143954247825794d0dc5c0d0e3d98365} 
std\+::pair$<$ double, double $>$ Tri\+Quad\+Pol\+::line\+In\+Polar (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{double}]{theta1}{, }\item[{double}]{rho1}{, }\item[{double}]{theta2}{, }\item[{double}]{rho2}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Straight-\/line segment between two vertices written in polar form about pos\+In\+Plane. 

For a line through $(\theta_1,\rho_1)$ and $(\theta_2,\rho_2)$ in polar coords around pos\+In\+Plane, returns $(\theta_0,\rho_0)$ such that $ r(\theta) = d_0 / cos(\theta − \theta_0)$ on that segment.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em theta1,rho1} & Endpoint 1 in polar coords. \\
\hline
{\em theta2,rho2} & Endpoint 2 in polar coords. \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
Pair $\{\theta_0, d_0\}$.
\end{DoxyReturn}
Return line parameters (θ0,d0) for the edge between two vertices in polar coordinates.

To avoid degeneracy when θ1 == θ2, a tiny perturbation is applied.

\Hypertarget{classTriQuadPol_aea4deef1068d5095c5c6afbb741be8d7}\index{TriQuadPol@{TriQuadPol}!transformInPlane@{transformInPlane}}
\index{transformInPlane@{transformInPlane}!TriQuadPol@{TriQuadPol}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{transformInPlane()}{transformInPlane()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classTriQuadPol_aea4deef1068d5095c5c6afbb741be8d7} 
void Tri\+Quad\+Pol\+::transform\+In\+Plane (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Build an orthonormal basis on the triangle plane and project (vertices, point). 

Sets {\ttfamily verts\+In\+Plane} and {\ttfamily pos\+In\+Plane} in a local (e1,e2) frame with vertex 0 at (0,0).

Builds an orthonormal basis (e1 along edge 0-\/\texorpdfstring{$>$}{>}1, e2 = e3 x e1, e3 normal), then expresses vertices and the observation point in that basis. 

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{TriQuadPol_8cpp_source_l00139}{139}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{TriQuadPol_8cpp_source}{Tri\+Quad\+Pol.\+cpp}}.
